feed.title: Scholar Alerts — Clean Feed
feed.subtitle: Google Scholar alerts reformatted as individual article entries

entry.title: Polarimetry in Planetary Sciences and Astronomy
entry.content:
Authors: CH Patty, J Grone, BO Demory, J Kühn, J Ma, W Mulder... - arXiv preprint arXiv ..., 2026

Abstract: ... and the very dim but polarized light reflected by an exoplanet . In this paper, we discuss and review ... In section 4 we describe the studies carried out on using polarimetry to detect biosignatures . The ... will be very useful to image the surface of ...

Tags: Reviewer, Too: Astrobiology, Reviewer, Too: Exoplanets

entry.title: René Doyon| Université de Montréal, Département de physique, Trottier Institute for Research on Exoplanet and Observatoire du Mont Mégantic
entry.content:
Authors: PXI Medal, C Pio IV, XIV Leo, XVI Benedict...

Abstract: ... We make an exception to include the temperate mini- Neptune K2 - 18b as a potential habitable world, despite its mass uncertainty of 18%. ... is more likely to be due to life or a planetary process. K2 - 18b serves as a reminder that the search for ...

Tags: Reviewer, Too: Astrobiology, Reviewer, Too: Exoplanets

entry.title: The Grammar of Emergence
entry.content:
Authors: CF Dietz

Abstract: ... Stuart Kauffman has spent five decades working out the details of this grammar: the mathematics of catalytic closure, the thermodynamics of constraint closure, the expansion logic of the adjacent possible, the organizational character of Kantian ...

Tags: Reviewer, Too: Origin of Life, Reviewer, too: Language, Numeracy, and Musicality, Reviewer, too: Complexity

entry.title: Engineering pH and thermostability of β-mannanase from Aspergillus niger for simplified and direct production of manno-oligosaccharides from guar split

author.name: origins of life"|"origin of life"|"emergence of ...

entry.content:
Authors: H Liu, Q Yao, D Sun, C Wu, Q Yan, Y Li, Z Jiang - Bioresource Technology, 2026

Abstract: ... In vitro fermentation assays demonstrated its superior prebiotic activity, stimulating the growth of various Lactobacillus and Bifidobacterium strains more effectively than partially hydrolyzed guar gum or fructo-oligosaccharide. This study ...

entry.title: Prebiotics and Gut Microbiota: Role in Maintaining Normal Human Physiology

author.name: origins of life"|"origin of life"|"emergence of ...

entry.content:
Authors: S Bibi, Z Nisa, A Ahmed, M Tuaseen, M Ibrahim...

Abstract: ... This review article examines the development of the prebiotic concept, lists the different kinds of prebiotics, such as fructans, galacto-oligosaccharides, xylo-oligosaccharides, chitooligosaccharides, lactulose, resistant starch, and polyphenols, and explains ...

entry.title: Photochemistry and haze in chemically reducing terrestrial atmospheres

author.name: origins of life"|"origin of life"|"emergence of ...

entry.content:
Authors: L Gu, S Fan - Fundamental Research, 2026

Abstract: ... In summary, the secondary atmosphere of the prebiotic Earth is potentially chemically reducing. Abundant reducing gases have likely contributed to the production of prebiotic compounds and to keeping the planet sufficiently warm ...

entry.content:

Abstract: ... The origin of life is the origin of the first biological closure. The moment when some subset of the prebiotic chemistry first began constituting ... The dominant framework for understanding this transition has been the RNA world hypothesis: a ...

entry.title: ... Recovery from Rapeseed Meal via Conventional and Enzyme-Assisted Extraction Techniques: Toward Emerging Prebiotic Pectic Oligosaccharide Development

author.name: origins of life"|"origin of life"|"emergence of ...

entry.content:
Authors: K Banjanac, M Veljković, M Simović, A Tomić... - Foods, 2026

Abstract: This study investigates the extraction of pectic polysaccharides from rapeseed meal (RSM) using both conventional and enzyme-assisted techniques, and the obtained pectic polysaccharide fractions will be used later to produce prebiotic pectic ...

Abstract: The study investigated the dose-dependent protective effects of xylooligosaccharides (XOS) against metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease (MASLD) in mice, focusing on gut microbiota and microbial metabolites ...
